title: pericalia pudica
intro: Pericalia pudica, also known as the sensitive plant or touch-me-not, is a perennial plant native to South America and parts of Asia, characterized by its compound leaves that fold up when touched. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pericalia pudica include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Pericalia pudica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies for its perceived health benefits.
